'''Graeme Hallas''' (born 27 February 1971) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in non-[[Test match (rugby league)|Test match]]es, and at club level for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Hull FC]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[York City Knights]] as a {{rlp|wg|ce}}, or {{rlp|lf}},<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and coached at club level for [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]].<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Graeme Hallas was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Hallas began his amateur career with [[Bradford Dudley Hill|Dudley Hill]] ARLFC.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=Dave|title=Rugby League: Hallas to return as an amateur|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-hallas-to-return-as-an-amateur-1550776.html|website=The Independent|accessdate=20 May 2015|date=11 September 1992}}</ref> He played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and was signed by [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in October 1992 in exchange for Rob Hutchinson and a fee of £70,000.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Wigan must be wary of Broncos' enthusiasm and flair: Tonight's World Club Challenge at Central Park is likely to be fast and open |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-wigan-must-be-wary-of-broncos-enthusiasm-and-flair-tonight-s-world-club-challenge-at-central-park-is-likely-to-be-fast-and-open-1560500.html |website=The Independent |access-date=25 June 2022 |date=30 October 1992}}</ref> He also played in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull F.C.]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]].

Despite Huddersfield Giants being relegated from the Super League in 2001, Hallas opted to stay with the club and signed a new contract.<ref>{{cite web|title=McNamara agrees new Giants deal|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/1588834.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=20 May 2015|date=10 October 2001}}</ref> In 2002, Hallas played in Huddersfield Giants' victory over [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Northern Ford Premiership]] Grand Final.<ref>{{cite web|title=Giants bounce back in style|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/2319689.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=20 May 2015|date=12 October 2002}}</ref> He left the club at the end of the season and joined the newly formed [[York City Knights]]. He appeared 22 times for the club in the 2003 season, scoring nine tries.<ref>{{cite web|title=Appearances/Scorers - Season 2003|url=http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances03.htm|publisher=York City Knights|accessdate=20 May 2015|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20060928194616/http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances03.htm|archivedate=28 September 2006}}</ref>

===International honours===
Hallas was selected for the [[1992 Great Britain Lions tour of Australia and New Zealand]].

==Coaching career==
Hallas was appointed as head coach at [[Hunslet Hawks]]. He resigned in July 2009.<ref>{{cite web |title=Hunslet Hawks coach Hallas quits |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8128397.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=25 June 2022 |date=1 July 2009}}</ref>
